claim: AI copilots (embedded tool suggestions in workflows) are ineffective; AI agents that own entire customer interaction funnels and make decisions end-to-end outperform them 1.5x+ over humans
actionable: Replace copilot-style tools with fully autonomous agents that own complete workflows; measure agent performance weekly and iterate on prompts and contracts rather than stopping at human parity.
source_title: AI Copilots Are a Dead End. Here's What Actually Works | Kavak CEO

claim: Build a shared ontology of business rules and data definitions before deploying agents; agents must communicate with one another through agreed-upon schemas to handle edge cases at scale
actionable: Before building agents, spend 2-4 weeks documenting your business rules, data types, and decision points in a shared schema (e.g., JSON); agents read and write to this ontology.
source_title: AI Copilots Are a Dead End. Here's What Actually Works | Kavak CEO

claim: Customer experience will degrade for 6-12 months during agent transition; don't hire humans back; instead, improve agent prompts and add capability layers daily until parity then surpass
actionable: Expect performance dips when transitioning from humans to agents; set 12-month targets and commit to daily prompt iteration; never backfill with humans once the agent transition starts.
source_title: AI Copilots Are a Dead End. Here's What Actually Works | Kavak CEO

claim: Test agent performance against rubrics weekly, not quarterly; iterate on prompts daily based on failure traces to improve specific weak areas (e.g., 'agent undershoots scope')
actionable: Run weekly eval passes scoring agents on 4-6 criteria; use traces to identify top 3 failure modes; update system prompts and add few-shot examples targeting those modes.
source_title: AI Copilots Are a Dead End. Here's What Actually Works | Kavak CEO

claim: Don't build for current models (GPT-4, Claude 3.5); build for next-gen (GPT-7, Claude 5); simplify harness now so you're ready when faster/cheaper models arrive in 12-18 months
actionable: When designing agent system architecture, assume models improve 2-3x in capability and 10x in cost/speed; design for simplicity so you can leverage future models without rework.
source_title: AI Copilots Are a Dead End. Here's What Actually Works | Kavak CEO

claim: Humans with domain expertise should do QA on agents until agents reach parity; then prioritize agent scale over edge case coverage (80% handle 95% of cases)
actionable: Have domain experts write failing test traces from production; use these to improve agent prompts; once parity achieved, focus on throughput not perfection on rare cases.
source_title: AI Copilots Are a Dead End. Here's What Actually Works | Kavak CEO

claim: Kavak manages 90-95% of customer interactions with agents; pricing, underwriting, reconditioning handled by data-driven systems; agents orchestrate these systems in real-time
actionable: Use agents as orchestrators on top of specialized subsystems (pricing models, ML pipelines); agents decide which system to invoke and handle handoff logic, reducing need for general-purpose agent capability.
source_title: AI Copilots Are a Dead End. Here's What Actually Works | Kavak CEO

claim: Operator must read failing agent traces weekly and manually update prompts; don't expect agents to self-improve; operator pattern-recognition is how you close gaps
actionable: Schedule 4-6 hours per week to read agent failure traces; document patterns you see; write 2-3 few-shot examples and add them to agent system prompt.
source_title: AI Copilots Are a Dead End. Here's What Actually Works | Kavak CEO

claim: Build vertically integrated infrastructure below your primary product so agents have reliable subsystems to orchestrate (e.g., Kavak owns logistics, financing, reconditioning)
actionable: When deploying customer-facing agents, build data-driven subsystems (pricing, logistics, inventory) first; agents become orchestrators, not workarounds for missing tools.
source_title: AI Copilots Are a Dead End. Here's What Actually Works | Kavak CEO

claim: Use an operating system/dispatcher layer to route edge cases: classify incoming request, check if agent can handle, else route to human or specialized system (e.g., fraud detection)
actionable: Build a rule-based router above your agents; classify requests into 5-10 categories and assign to appropriate handler (agent, human, specialized ML system); iterate routing rules weekly.
source_title: AI Copilots Are a Dead End. Here's What Actually Works | Kavak CEO

claim: Founders: focus on solving real problems users need, not nice-to-haves, especially in emerging markets; build with 15+ year vision (90% of value created after year 15)
actionable: When evaluating startup ideas, ask: would users die/suffer without this? If not, it's a want not a need; for LatAm markets, focus on necessity problems; plan for 15+ year compound of 1% improvements.
source_title: AI Copilots Are a Dead End. Here's What Actually Works | Kavak CEO

claim: In emerging markets, you must build 4+ parallel sub-businesses (logistics, payment rails, legal compliance) underneath your core product; don't assume infrastructure exists
actionable: Before launching in LatAm, audit which US infrastructure (payment processors, shipping, financing) is unavailable; budget 6-12 months to build custom versions of each missing piece.
source_title: AI Copilots Are a Dead End. Here's What Actually Works | Kavak CEO

claim: Zoom in/out daily: zoom in on the micro (next 24 hours, one decision, one friction point to remove); zoom out monthly on whether you're building for the right 15-year vision
actionable: Each morning, identify one user friction to remove that day; each Sunday, check if your week's micro-decisions compound toward your 15-year vision; adjust weekly if diverging.
source_title: AI Copilots Are a Dead End. Here's What Actually Works | Kavak CEO

claim: Fire yourself annually: ask what CEO the company needs, what risks they face, what to protect, then ask if you can hire yourself back; let go of old personas to level up
actionable: Once a year, write down what the company needs from its CEO, what you need to let go of, what new skills you need to build; assess honestly if you can grow into that role.
source_title: AI Copilots Are a Dead End. Here's What Actually Works | Kavak CEO

claim: Risk is poorly assessed by most founders (doomsday bias); instead, make a decision, assess results in 24 hours, iterate; most outcomes are recoverable if you show up daily
actionable: Stop catastrophizing decisions; commit to reviewing each decision within 24 hours; if it's wrong, adjust; don't let decisions compound without daily re-assessment.
source_title: AI Copilots Are a Dead End. Here's What Actually Works | Kavak CEO
